RT by @AdamSchefter: NEWS: NFL teams received a letter today about Caleb Banks as he recovers from a surgically repaired bone in his left foot. Per ESPN sources, the letter states that after a CT scan on Tuesday that Banks is “on pace to be fully cleared for full football activities in early June.”
RT by @AdamSchefter: Stephen Jones said the team has no assurances as to when or if George Pickens will show for the offseason program. Pickens has to sign the tender in order to show up.

Cowboys EVP Stephen Jones told reporters today that they’ve decided WR George Pickens will play on the franchise tag this season, and there won’t be talks on a long-term deal.

Pickens has not signed the tag yet.

Seahawks GM John Schneider told reporters this week that, with Seattle entering this draft with only four picks, he would like to trade back from the team’s No. 32 selection in the first round to accumulate more picks. The Seahawks are hardly the only team near the end of the first round willing to move back; others are as well.

A moth walks into a lawyer's office:

Moth: "Hey how are you doing?"

Lawyer: "Oh hey moth. What can I help you with?"

Moth: "Where do I begin. I'm a general manager for an NFL team. Billion dollar franchise. We have needs all over the place. No quarterback of the present or future, but no draft capital to get a good one. Running back room is a mess. One good wide receiver. Been rebuidling the offensive line for years. And the defense? Don't even get me started. The roster as a whole is a disgrace, and frankly there's nobody to point the finger at other than me. The fans understandably do this, and I just wake up every day wondering what's the point anymore. My children go to school and get ridiculed. My wife can't leave the house without someone chirping at her. My entire job and life is a charade and facade."

Lawyer: "That's really unfortunate moth, but it sounds like you should be talking to a psychologist or some sort of mental health professional. I'm a lawyer. Why did you come in here?"

Moth: "Because the light was on."

And people really wonder why EVERYBODY outside of Oklahoma complains about this team. 

The Thunder really bad for basketball

Devin Booker CALLS OUT the refs for this play: 

“I heard Caruso tell them to call the tech and he ended up doing it. In my 11 years, I haven't called a ref out by name, but James Williams was terrible tonight. It's bad for the sport. People are going to start viewing this as the WWE if they're not held responsible.”
Referees after leading the OKC Thunder to another victory
All NBA referees are issued one of these before every OKC Thunder game

Dillon Brooks PISSED about the officiating after Game 2 loss to OKC:

“Ya'll should be interviewing the officials." 

"That should be a new thing in the NBA. Officials got to  explain themselves because it's getting ridiculous when you can see it. It starts getting fiery. No control out there. Now they're just whistling on one side."

Devin Booker just WENT OFF on the NBA refs in the post game press conference:

"It's definitely something that has to be looked at. I heard (Alex) Caruso tell them to call the tech and he ended up doing it. In my 11 years, I haven't called a ref out by name, but James (Williams) was terrible tonight, through and through. It's bad for the sport, bad for the integrity of the sport. People are going to start viewing this as the WWE if they're not held responsible."

"I know I haven't won a championship in this league but I have been in it for 11 years now, so to get to this point to be treated like that, for me to even be saying something out loud, it's bad. It's my first time in 11 years but it's needed. Whatever, I get fined for it, everybody can pull the clips and see where the frustration comes from."

(via @DuaneRankin)

LeBron vs Kevin Durant is 🍿

After KD missed Game 1, Game 2  lived up to every bit of the hype.

We finally witnessed these two legends go at it in a playoff series that’s NOT the NBA Finals, in their first postseason meeting since 2018.

As of now, they've played 47 games against each other. Let's break it down:

🏀 LeBron: 27 total wins
🏀 KD: 20 total wins

Regular season:

🏀 LeBron: 21-11 vs KD
🏀 KD: 11-21 vs LeBron

Playoffs:

🏀 LeBron: 6-9 vs KD
🏀 KD: 9-6 vs LeBron

Those 14 playoff games spanned 3 NBA Finals, with Durant going 8-1 against James with the Warriors, and 1-4 with OKC.

KD averages more, with 29 PPG in their matchups, while LeBron is at 27.7 PPG.

And they recently made history together.

On Christmas Day this season when they played, KD and LeBron broke the record for most combined points in a head-to-head matchup in NBA history...

...with 76,630 combined points heading into that game.

Now, we get a whole playoff series between them once again.

KD is 37. LeBron is 41. 

This may be one of the last times we see them match up in the postseason.

The King.
The Slim Reaper.
This is gonna be fun

RT by @WorldWideWob: holy fuck, a hair dryer at a Paris airport broke Polymarket weather markets & made someone $34,000 richer

- polymarket was settling Paris temperature bets on a single Météo France sensor sitting near the Charles de Gaulle runway perimeter - basically unguarded

- the guy bought the long-shot outcome (like "22°C" when everyone expected 18°C) for pennies, since nobody thought it'd hit

- then he walked up to the probe and briefly heated the air around it with a portable heat source, spiking the reading just long enough to register as the daily max

- temperature snapped back to normal in minutes, the market resolved in his favor, and he cashed out - twice, on April 6 and April 15, before Météo France caught on and filed charges
